title: grok-prompts v1.2.0
description: Official JSON Schema for grok-prompts.yaml — defines a reusable, versioned prompt library with variable interpolation, per-prompt model overrides, and tagging. Compatible with grok-install.yaml@1.0+ and grok@2026.4+.

version.description: Semantic version of this prompt library file.

author.description: X handle of the library maintainer, prefixed with '@'.

compatibility.description: Platform specs this file is tested and compatible with.

prompt_library.description: Named collection of reusable prompt templates. Each key becomes the prompt identifier used in '@grok use prompts:<id>' triggers.

template.description: The prompt text. Use {variable_name} placeholders for runtime interpolation.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this prompt does and when to use it.

variables.description: List of required variable names that must be supplied when invoking this prompt. Must match placeholders used in the template.

optional_variables.description: Variable names that may optionally be supplied to enrich the prompt.

temperature.description: Per-prompt sampling temperature override. Overrides the global grok.temperature setting.

max_tokens.description: Per-prompt token cap override. Useful for prompts that need longer or shorter outputs.

model.description: Per-prompt model override. Use when this prompt requires a specific model capability.

tags.description: Categorisation tags for filtering and discovery (e.g. 'social', 'code', 'marketing').

output_format.description: Expected structure of the prompt output.

system_prompt.description: Static system-level instruction prepended before the template. Must be static text — never interpolate user-supplied values here.

reasoning_mode.description: Extended thinking budget for this prompt. 'max' produces highest-quality reasoning but increases latency and cost significantly.

response_format.description: Structured output format constraint. 'json_object' enforces valid JSON; 'json_schema' additionally validates against a declared schema.

cache_responses.description: Cache the response for identical variable combinations to reduce latency and cost.
